policies.title: Principle: Be Safe
policies.fix: Do not pose security threats, exceed needed data access, or abuse store/network.

policies.title: Principle: Be Honest
policies.fix: Disclose all functionality clearly. Avoid deceptive or clickbait behavior.

policies.title: Principle: Be Useful
policies.fix: Provide a unique, high-quality, useful experience.

policies.title: Best Practices and Guidelines
policies.fix: Review the Chrome Web Store best-practices guide before submitting.

policies.title: Mature & Sexually Explicit Material
policies.fix: Remove pornographic or sexually explicit content. Mature content needs a strict gating mechanism.

policies.title: Malicious and Prohibited Products
policies.fix: Remove malware, spyware, cryptocurrency miners, paywall circumvention, and similar prohibited code.

policies.title: Hate Speech and Violent Behavior
policies.fix: Remove hate speech, harassment, or content inciting violence.

policies.title: Regulated Goods and Services
policies.fix: Restrict and license features for firearms, gambling, alcohol, tobacco, drugs, or financial services.

policies.title: Privacy Policy required
policies.fix: Provide a privacy policy URL in your store listing if you handle user data.

policies.title: Limited Use of user data
policies.fix: Use user data only for the disclosed user-facing feature; no transferring or selling for unrelated purposes.

policies.title: Use of Permissions - minimum necessary
policies.fix: Limit host permissions to specific origins. Remove every permission you do not actively use.

policies.title: Disclosure Requirements (in-product disclosure + privacy policy)
policies.fix: Disclose data collection in-product and link to a privacy policy.

policies.title: Handling Requirements (encryption, secure transmission)
policies.fix: Encrypt user data at rest and in transit. Replace all plaintext http:// endpoints with HTTPS.

policies.title: Impersonation & Intellectual Property
policies.fix: Do not impersonate other publishers or use their trademarks/copyrighted assets without permission.

policies.title: Deceptive Installation Tactics
policies.fix: Do not bundle or trick users into installing your extension.

policies.title: Accepting Payment From Users
policies.fix: Use a transparent, secure payment flow and disclose all charges up front.

policies.title: Misleading or Unexpected Behavior
policies.fix: Behavior must match the listing description; do not change settings without consent.

policies.title: Ads policy
policies.fix: Ads must not be the primary purpose, must be clearly identified, and must not be injected into pages without consent.

policies.title: Affiliate Ads policy
policies.fix: Affiliate links must be a result of a clear user action and a disclosed feature.

policies.title: Featured Products requirements
policies.fix: Follow the Featured Products checklist (manifest V3, polished UX, etc.) for promotion eligibility.

policies.title: Spam and Abuse
policies.fix: Do not stuff keywords, submit duplicate extensions, or manipulate ratings/installs.

policies.title: Quality Guidelines
policies.fix: Provide a polished UI, accurate metadata, working features, and reasonable performance.

policies.title: Listing Requirements (icons, screenshots, descriptions)
policies.fix: Provide an icons block including 128x128 PNG plus accurate screenshots and description in the listing.

policies.title: Minimum Functionality
policies.fix: Provide meaningful, useful functionality beyond a thin wrapper around a webpage.

policies.title: Chrome Apps deprecation policy
policies.fix: Migrate legacy Chrome Apps to Extensions. Chrome Apps are deprecated on most platforms.

policies.title: Code Readability Requirements (no obfuscation)
policies.fix: Submit readable, reviewable source. Minification is allowed; deliberate obfuscation is not.

policies.title: API Use - intended purpose only
policies.fix: Use Chrome and Web Platform APIs only for their documented purposes.

policies.title: Additional Requirements for Manifest V3
policies.fix: Set manifest_version to 3. Move background pages to a service worker and stop using remotely hosted code.

policies.title: 2-Step Verification required for publisher account
policies.fix: Enable 2-Step Verification on the Google account that publishes to the Chrome Web Store.

policies.title: Reported rejection: violates single-purpose policy (multiple unrelated features)
policies.fix: Limit the extension to a single, narrow purpose. Split additional features into separate listings.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Blue Argon — disclosure mismatch / does not match listing
policies.fix: Ensure your store description, screenshots and Permission Justifications accurately describe every feature in the package.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Purple Agate — affiliate links / advertising not disclosed
policies.fix: Disclose any affiliate codes, referral links, or ad injection in the listing description and privacy practices.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Red Invalid — manifest references files not in the package
policies.fix: Verify every path in manifest.json (background, content_scripts, web_accessible_resources, icons, action.default_popup) exists in the zip.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Yellow Magnesium — broad host permissions without need
policies.fix: Use activeTab or specific host patterns. Justify each host permission in the developer dashboard.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Green Terpene — code obfuscated / minified beyond what build tools produce
policies.fix: Do not obfuscate code. Standard minification is allowed; control-flow obfuscation, hex-name renaming, and string-array packers are rejected.

policies.title: Reported rejection: Grey Epsilon — privacy policy missing or inadequate
policies.fix: Provide a privacy policy URL on the dashboard whenever you handle personal/sensitive data, and answer all data-handling certifications.

policies.title: Reported rejection: keyword spam / misleading metadata
policies.fix: Remove superlatives and irrelevant trending keywords from the name. Review the title for trademark misuse.

policies.title: Reported rejection: missing reviewer test instructions / login
policies.fix: Provide working test credentials and step-by-step usage notes in "Notes for reviewers" when features require sign-in or backend access.
